channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Strike Kills Three Lebanese Soldiers Including Two Officers
item.description: An Israeli strike on a military vehicle in southern Lebanon killed two Lebanese army officers and one soldier, with the Israeli military saying it is investigating the incident amid ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: ✈️ Global Airlines Grapple With Fuel Price Spike at Rio Summit
item.description: Airline chiefs meeting in Rio de Janeiro are dealing with higher fuel costs from the Iran war by raising fares and tightening capacity, as the industry faces uncertainty over energy prices.

item.title: ✈️ Embraer Sees Airlines Delaying Plane Purchase Options Amid Iran War
item.description: Some airlines are delaying decisions on aircraft purchase options due to uncertainties from the Iran war, which has pushed up jet fuel prices, Embraer CEO Francisco Gomes Neto told Reuters.

item.title: ✈️ Airbus Nearing Widebody Order From Scandinavian Airlines
item.description: Airbus is close to a widebody order from SAS AB including A330neo and A350 aircraft, with 15-20 planes under consideration, Bloomberg News reported on Saturday.

item.title: 🚀 Musk SpaceX IPO Draws European Retail Investor Interest
item.description: European retail investors are among those jockeying for a piece of the anticipated SpaceX initial public offering, though observers warn the deal could prove bumpy for those without institutional resources.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Five Killed in Russian Attacks on Southern Kherson Region
item.description: Russian forces killed five civilians in three separate attacks on Ukraine southern Kherson region on Friday, as fighting continues despite ongoing ceasefire discussions.

item.title: 🏛️ Trump Faces Growing Republican Resistance as Midterm Pressures Mount
item.description: Republican lawmakers are showing increased willingness to break with Trump on key initiatives as midterm election pressures intensify, with both parties skeptical of claims of an actual revolt.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Maine Senate Candidate Platner Faces Intimidation Allegations
item.description: Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner denies reports of physical intimidation toward women as scandals shadow the Maine Democratic primary race.

item.title: 💰 US Explores Redirecting Frozen Iranian Assets to Gulf Allies for War Damage Repairs
item.description: The Trump administration is assessing how to steer frozen Iranian assets toward helping Gulf allies rebuild from damage inflicted during the three-month Iran-US conflict.

item.title: 🇹🇼 US Envoy: Taiwan Must Spend Smarter on Defense, Learn from Ukraine and Middle East
item.description: A senior US diplomat urged Taiwan to optimize defense spending and adopt drone warfare lessons from Ukraine and Middle East conflicts to maintain the military balance with China.

item.title: 🇫🇷 French Telecom Consortium Signs $23.4 Billion Deal to Buy SFR from Altice
item.description: Bouygues Telecom, Orange, and Free-iliad signed an agreement to acquire telecom operator SFR from Altice France in one of Europe largest telecom deals.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Secret Service-Level Security as Trump to Attend NBA Finals Game 3
item.description: Road closures, bag bans and extensive security measures will greet fans at Madison Square Garden as President Trump attends Game 3 of the NBA Finals between the Knicks and Spurs.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea Will Never Abandon Nuclear Status, Kim Yo Jong Warns
item.description: Kim Jong Un's sister declared North Korea will never retreat from its nuclear-armed status, warning the country will not tolerate any threats amid ongoing tensions with the US and South Korea.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US Strikes Iranian Radar Sites After Drone Launch Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites after shooting down four Iranian attack drones launched toward the Strait of Hormuz, marking another escalation in the fragile ceasefire between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇮🇱 France and Allies Prepare National Sanctions to Pressure Israel Over West Bank
item.description: France is coordinating with several countries to impose national sanctions on Israel over its West Bank policies, as diplomatic pressure mounts over settlement expansion and the treatment of Palestinians.

item.title: 🇧🇷✈️ Brazilian Airline Azul Cuts Flights Amid Fuel Shock From Iran Conflict
item.description: Brazil's Azul airline announced further capacity cuts due to soaring jet fuel prices linked to the Iran war, as the aviation industry worldwide grapples with energy costs driven by Middle East tensions.

item.title: 🇫🇷 Former French First Lady Bernadette Chirac Dies at 93
item.description: Bernadette Chirac, the steel-willed former first lady who spent 12 years at the Élysée Palace beside President Jacques Chirac, has died. She was known for weathering political challenges and her husband's infidelities with dignity.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Strikes St Petersburg After Putin Rejects Zelensky Meeting
item.description: Ukrainian drones targeted St Petersburg after Putin rejected a proposed meeting with Zelensky. The attacks came on the final day of the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum, with one person killed and an oil depot set ablaze.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India's Viral "Cockroach" Youth Movement Holds First Major Protest
item.description: Hundreds joined a protest led by India's mock "Cockroach Janta Party," a youth movement with roughly 22 million Instagram followers that has become the largest online expression of dissent against the government's policies.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Missing US Student Found Dead in Japan
item.description: The body of James Higginbotham, 20, was discovered in a mountainous area near Kyoto after he disappeared while on a family trip to Japan. Local authorities are investigating the circumstances of his death.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Claims Interception of 339 Ukrainian Drones Over 13 Hours
item.description: Russian Defence Ministry reports its air defenses intercepted and destroyed 339 Ukrainian drones across multiple regions including Moscow in a 13-hour period, marking one of the largest drone attacks on Russian territory.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Strikes in Lebanon Kill 12 Including Senior Military Officers
item.description: Israeli attacks in Lebanon killed 12 people including a brigadier general and captain, escalating tensions along the Israel-Lebanon border amid ongoing regional conflict.

item.title: ⛽ US Energy Secretary: Lower Gas Prices Require Resolution With Iran
item.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated that lowering pump prices will ultimately require a resolution with Iran to restore oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, linking domestic energy costs to the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇺 Cuba's Raul Castro Makes First Public Appearance Since US Indictment
item.description: Former Cuban leader Raul Castro appeared at an Interior Ministry event in Havana, his first public appearance since being indicted on murder charges in the United States last month.

item.title: 🇳🇿 US Approves $1.5 Billion Seahawk Helicopter Sale to New Zealand
item.description: The United States approved the sale of five maritime helicopters to New Zealand worth $1.5 billion, as Wellington moves to nearly double its military spending amid growing regional security concerns.
